title: handling of register arrays broken after bdb5d45591d7501825349bedcb2952d0b80a1112
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.44+60 (git sha1 bdb5d4559, g++ 13.2.0-23ubuntu4 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

[sc_issue_simon_cipher_job0_floorplan0_20241106-014731.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17651881/sc_issue_simon_cipher_job0_floorplan0_20241106-014731.tar.gz)

```
tar xvf sc_issue_simon_cipher_job0_floorplan0_20241106-014731.tar.gz
cd sc_issue_simon_cipher_job0_floorplan0_20241106-014731
./run.sh
```

From: https://github.com/siliconcompiler/siliconcompiler/issues/2965

### Expected Behavior

Correctly handling the register array (as before bdb5d45591d7501825349bedcb2952d0b80a1112).

### Actual Behavior

Before bdb5d45591d7501825349bedcb2952d0b80a1112 this netlist handled `reg [31:0] key_schedule [0:43];` correctly, afterwards the `proc` call in `synth` appears to be incomplete.
```
yosys> opt_clean

4.5. Executing OPT_CLEAN pass (remove unused cells and wires).
Warning: Ignoring module simon_cipher because it contains processes (run 'proc' command first).
```
the resultant netlist ends with a bunch of unmapped instantances
```

            "$_ALDFFE_PPP_": 32,
            "$_AND_": 438,
            "$_MUX_": 2554,
            "$_NOT_": 414,
            "$_OR_": 524,
            "$_XOR_": 296,
            "$scopeinfo": 2,
```
code_blocks:
yosys> opt_clean

4.5. Executing OPT_CLEAN pass (remove unused cells and wires).
Warning: Ignoring module simon_cipher because it contains processes (run 'proc' command first).
